Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The click on the TL center had no impact on gameplay - it was a no_impact action. The animation showed changes in 4 corner regions (32,32), (32,56), (56,32), (56,56) - these are the gray border corner pieces around the BR grid. So clicking the TL center only changed the HUD/border elements, not the actual gameplay.
Let me think about what to click instead. Maybe I need to click on the colored cells (the 6x6 tiles) rather than the center pieces. Or maybe I need to click on the BR grid itself.
Let me try clicking on one of the colored cells in the BR grid, or on the border/frame around it. Or maybe I should try clicking on cells in the other grids.
Let me try clicking on a red cell in the BR grid area, or on the border. Actually, the BR grid is all blue, so maybe I need to click on cells to change their color.
Let me try clicking on a cell in the BR grid to see if it changes.

sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Clicking TL center had no gameplay impact - only changed border decorations
# Let me try clicking on a colored cell in the BR grid instead
# BR grid cells are at rows 36,44,52 and cols 36,44,52
# Let me click the top-left blue cell of BR grid at (36, 36)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 36}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
